Scenario: Verify service request page loads successfully
Precondition: User is logged in with valid credentials
Steps: 1. Login with valid credentials\n2. Navigate to service request page
Expected Result: Service request page loads with heading "Service Requests"
Actual Result: Page loaded at /service-request/ with h1 "Service Requests"

Scenario: Verify user can create inside user service request
Precondition: User is logged in and on service request page
Steps:
1. Click +Create Service Request
2. Enter title
3. Enter description
4. Select inside user type
5. Select priority
6. Select project site
7. Click Save
Expected Result: New service request is created successfully
Actual Result: +Create Service Request button clicked, form filled, Save clicked with force=True
Remarks: force=True used to bypass modal overlay

Scenario: Verify user can create outsource service request
Precondition: User is logged in and on service request page
Steps:
1. Click +Create Service Request
2. Enter title
3. Enter description
4. Select outsource user type
5. Assign to outside user
6. Click Save
Expected Result: Outsource service request is created and assigned to outside user
Actual Result: Outsource option selected, form filled, Save clicked

Scenario: Verify user can edit a service request
Steps: 1. Click edit/pencil icon\n2. Modify title\n3. Click Save
Expected Result: Service request is updated successfully
Actual Result: No edit button found on service request cards
Remarks: Edit button not visible in current UI

Scenario: Verify service request list displays all requests
Steps: 1. Navigate to service request page\n2. Observe the listing
Expected Result: All service requests are listed with title
Actual Result: description
column10: 12+ service request cards found with h3 elements (e.g. "Service request-rohith,ivan", "cerin service request")

Scenario: Verify related request can be specified when pausing

Scenario: Verify resume button enables only when related completed
Steps: 1. Check resume before related completion\n2. Complete related task\n3. Check resume again
Expected Result: Resume disabled until related task completed
Actual Result: Dependency logic not fully testable in single session

Scenario: Verify assigned user can resume a paused request
Steps: 1. Click Resume button on paused request
Expected Result: Status changes back to In Progress
Actual Result: Resume button found on paused requests (3 paused requests visible)

Scenario: Verify assigned user can complete a request
Steps: 1. Login as assigned user\n2. Navigate to service request\n3. Click Complete button
Expected Result: Status changes to Completed
